$schema_note: Shared cross-chain digest anchors for Materios intent-settlement. Each entry is a fixed input + expected blake2b_256 output. Team A's Rust pallet, Team B's Aiken validator, and Team C's TS SDK MUST ALL produce byte-for-byte identical outputs for each vector. If any side diverges, investigate all three before patching — silent drift will cause committee signature mismatches that look like 'voucher invalid' but are actually serialisation bugs.

reference_impls.rust: pallets/materios-intent-settlement/src/digests.rs (pending Team A follow-up PR for _with_address helper)

intt_empty_body._description: IntentId digest for an empty body. Blake2b-256 of b"INTT".

vchr_empty_body._description: Voucher digest for an empty body. Blake2b-256 of b"VCHR".

bfpr_empty_body._description: BatchFairnessProof digest for an empty body. Blake2b-256 of b"BFPR".

cmtt_one_member._description: Committee digest for a single-member committee with threshold = 1. Body = pubkey(32B) ++ u32_le(threshold).

clam_32_ones._description: ClaimId digest for a 32-byte body of all 0x11s. Blake2b-256 of b"CLAM" ++ 32*0x11.

voucher_digest_with_address._description: THREE-WAY CROSS-CHAIN ANCHOR for voucher bodies that include a serialised Cardano Address. The beneficiary_address_cbor_hex below is what Aiken's `builtin.serialise_data(Address)` produces in Plutus V3 for the given bech32 address. Rust must reproduce this via `pallas_codec::plutus_data::PlutusData::encode_cbor(&address_data)` with the same indefinite-length constr CBOR shape; TS must reproduce it via Lucid's `Data.to(address, Address)` encoder. If any encoder produces a different `beneficiary_address_cbor_hex`, every BatchClaimVoucher / RefundCredit / RefundDeposit tx will silently reject on-chain.
voucher_digest_with_address._address_shape: CIP-0019 type-0 (payment VK + stake VK inline)
voucher_digest_with_address._beneficiary_address_cbor_note: Plutus V3 CBOR: constr-0 of [constr-0(VK payment_hash), constr-0(constr-0(constr-0(VK stake_hash)))]. Uses INDEFINITE-LENGTH array encoding (0xd8799f...ff), NOT definite (0xd87982...). Length = 80 bytes.
voucher_digest_with_address._fee_lovelace_note: Not in the canonical voucher pre-image (fee is a separate keeper-fee redeemer field). Included here for Team A/C builder reference only.
voucher_digest_with_address._canonical_body_layout: claim_id(32B) || policy_id(32B) || beneficiary_address_cbor(80B) || amount_ada_u64_le(8B) || bfpr_digest(32B) || issued_block_u32_le(4B) || expiry_slot_cardano_u64_le(8B) = 196B total
voucher_digest_with_address._expected_hex_note: blake2b_256(tag_voucher ++ canonical_body) = blake2b_256(b"VCHR" ++ body). Aiken pinned source-of-truth: test_vectors.ak::vec_pinned_vchr_with_address.

teamA_realistic_vectors._note: Realistic test cases Team A's pallet uses — same Blake2b-256 primitive, different (more substantive) pre-image bodies. All 6 vectors reproduce byte-for-byte via hashlib.blake2b(digest_size=32).

intent_id._description: IntentKind::BuyPolicy variant. Pre-image: b'INTT' || submitter || nonce(LE64) || scale(kind) || submitted_block(LE32).

voucher_digest._description: Pre-image: b'VCHR' || claim_id || policy_id || scale(addr) || amount(LE64) || bfpr_digest || issued_block(LE32) || expiry_slot(LE64). Per §1.7, addr is SCALE-encoded as BoundedVec<u8> (compact-len prefix).
